stories_covered: Anthropic raises $30B at $900B valuation, Andrej Karpathy joins
Datadog up 31%, Figma accelerates to 50% growth
Cerebrus IPO smashes to $300, biggest US tech IPO since Snowflake
SpaceX announces June 12 IPO at $1.75T valuation raising $75B
Public market enthusiasm for AI companies, SaaS bottoming
harry_takes: AI companies need to hire thousands to avoid social unrest as the trend faces political resistance
Anthropic's valuation is justified at 18X ARR given their growth trajectory compared to peer multiples
Token spend at 4% of engineering payroll is table stakes and likely undercounting actual adoption across companies
The $1.75T SpaceX valuation is madness, pricing in pie-in-the-sky AI ambitions, though Elon has earned the right to make the bet
harry_boldest_quote: We see no signs that there's a short-term crash coming.
predictions: Jason: Salesforce token spend will increase 3-4X over the next two years as they move from 4% to potentially 12-15% of R&D budget
Rory: Cerebrus IPO success doesn't open the window for companies below that level; only mega companies can IPO in this environment
Jason: SaaS is no longer getting attention; public market focus has shifted entirely to AI companies and semiconductors
disagreements: Minor tension on whether SaaS recovery is durable: Jason more skeptical that underlying growth challenges remain despite short-term repricing, Rory willing to buy the reacceleration story for AI-attached companies.
harry_role: Harry moderates as traffic controller, asking pointed clarifying questions on valuation metrics and implications. He defers to Jason on fund mechanics and Rory on market positioning but challenges the SpaceX narrative directly.
notable: Harry claims Anthropic's approach to fundraising (clean $30B check) versus OpenAI's complexity (staged tranches, Amazon/SoftBank contingencies) reveals philosophical founder differences on capital timing.
